  • 使我見也當惑了 - Indicates the courtesans' allure, suggesting their ability to tempt others.
  • 遂叱放之 - Indicates a sudden change in the emperor's decision, suggesting his reaction to the courtesans' appearance.

[672]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 王道本乎人情 - Key philosophical statement meaning 'the way of kingship originates from human feelings.'
  • 不通人情，不能为帝王 - Concise statement meaning 'without understanding human feelings, one cannot be a king or emperor.'
  • 结发为夫妻 - Traditional idiom meaning 'to marry for the first time,' symbolizing the beginning of a marital union.
  • 恩爱两不疑 - Means 'mutual love without doubt,' expressing deep trust and affection.
  • 欢娱在今夕 - Means 'joy in this night,' emphasizing the fleeting nature of happiness.
  • 燕婉及良时 - Means 'graceful and delightful at the right time,' describing a harmonious and pleasant moment.
  • 征夫怀往路 - Means 'the traveler thinks of his journey ahead,' expressing the traveler's thoughts and concerns.
  • 起视夜何其 - Means 'rise and see what time it is,' indicating the traveler's awareness of time passing.
  • 参辰皆已没 - Means 'the stars of the constellation of Shen and Chen have both set,' symbolizing the end of a day.
  • 去去从此辞 - Means 'go away, parting from here,' expressing the final farewell.
  • 行役在战场 - Means 'the journey is on the battlefield,' indicating the harsh reality of military service.
  • 相见未有期 - Means 'no time to meet again,' expressing the uncertainty of future reunions.
  • 握手一长叹 - Means 'a long sigh upon parting,' expressing deep sorrow.
  • 泪为生别滋 - Means 'tears flow from the pain of separation,' emphasizing the emotional impact of parting.
  • 努力爱春华 - Means 'strive to cherish the spring blossoms,' encouraging the enjoyment of life's fleeting moments.
  • 莫忘欢乐时 - Means 'do not forget the times of joy,' reminding the wife to remember happy moments.
  • 生当复来归 - Means 'if I live, I will return,' expressing hope for a future reunion.
  • 死当长相思 - Means 'if I die, I will always think of you,' expressing eternal love even in death.
  • 足下胤子无恙 - Means 'your son is well,' addressing Ruan Ling and assuring him of the safety of his child.
  • 天或者启其情 - Means 'perhaps heaven has opened its heart,' suggesting divine intervention or fate.
  • 以延忠臣之世 - Means 'to extend the legacy of loyal ministers,' expressing the hope for continued loyalty and honor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 苏子卿 - Refers to Su Wu, a famous Han dynasty diplomat and envoy to the Xiongnu.
  • 匈奴 - Refers to the Xiongnu, a nomadic people who were often at odds with the Han dynasty.
  • 李陵 - Refers to Li Ling, a Han general who defected to the Xiongnu and wrote letters to Su Wu.
  • 燕王旦 - Refers to Yan Wang Dan, a Han prince who was involved in a rebellion against the Han court.
  • 元 - Refers to Su Wu's son, who was involved in a rebellion and was executed.
  • 胡妇子 - Refers to the son of Su Wu and a Xiongnu woman, who was later adopted as Su Wu's heir.
  • 王道 - Refers to the way of kingship, a concept in Confucianism emphasizing moral governance.
  • 人情 - Refers to human feelings and emotions, a central theme in Confucian ethics.

Proper Nouns

  • 苏子卿 - Su Wu, a Han dynasty diplomat and envoy to the Xiongnu.
  • 匈奴 - The Xiongnu, a nomadic people who were often at odds with the Han dynasty.
  • 李陵 - Li Ling, a Han general who defected to the Xiongnu and wrote letters to Su Wu.
  • 燕王旦 - Yan Wang Dan, a Han prince who was involved in a rebellion against the Han court.
  • 元 - Su Wu's son, who was involved in a rebellion and was executed.
  • 胡妇子 - The son of Su Wu and a Xiongnu woman, who was later adopted as Su Wu's heir.
  • 王道 - The way of kingship, a concept in Confucianism emphasizing moral governance.
  • 人情 - Human feelings and emotions, a central theme in Confucian ethics.

[673]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 胡澹庵十年贬海外 - Compound phrase meaning 'Hu Dan’an was exiled to overseas for ten years.' The term '贬海外' is a classical expression for exile to a distant land or overseas territory.
  • 北归之日 - 'On the day he returned north,' referring to his return from exile.
  • 饮于湘潭胡氏园 - 'Drank at Hu’s garden in Xiangtan,' a compound verb phrase indicating a social gathering or banquet.
  • 爱妓黎倩，留题壁间 - 'Loved the courtesan Li Qian and left a poem on the wall.' The phrase '留题壁间' is a common classical expression for inscribing poetry on a wall.
  • 君恩许归此一醉，旁有梨颊生微涡 - 'The emperor’s grace allows me this one drink,' and '梨颊生微涡' is a poetic description of a woman’s face, with '梨颊' meaning 'pear cheeks' (a classical term for a fair, rosy face), and '微涡' meaning 'a slight dimple.'
  • 十年浮海一身轻 - 'Ten years adrift at sea, my body now light as air.' The phrase '浮海' is a metaphor for exile or drifting overseas.
  • 归对梨涡却有情 - 'Returning to face the dimpled cheeks, yet still full of feeling.' This line continues the poetic imagery introduced earlier.
  • 世上无如人欲险 - 'In the world, nothing is more dangerous than human desire.' A philosophical statement.
  • 几人到此误平生 - 'How many have strayed from their lives because of this?' A rhetorical question.
  • 尝观东坡《志林》 - 'I once read Su Shi’s *Zhi Lin* (Record of Events).' *Zhi Lin* is a classical collection of anecdotes and reflections.
  • 张元忠之说 - 'Zhang Yuanzhong’s view,' referring to a historical figure’s commentary.
  • 苏子卿啮雪啖毡 - 'Su Ziqing bit snow and ate felt,' referring to the historical figure Su Bao (苏武), who was exiled to the northern frontier and endured harsh conditions.
  • 了死生之际 - 'Understood the boundary between life and death,' a philosophical expression.
  • 不免与胡妇生子 - 'Could not help but have a child with a Hu woman,' referring to a historical or literary anecdote.
  • 洞房绮绣之下 - 'In the chambers of luxury and beauty,' a poetic description of a luxurious setting.
  • 文公之论澹庵 - 'Master Wen’s discussion of Dan’an,' referring to Zhu Xi’s commentary on Hu Dan’an.
  • 郑叔友论刘、项 - 'Zheng Shuyou’s discussion of Liu Bang and Xiang Yu,' referring to the founding figures of the Han dynasty.
  • 项王有吞岳渎意气 - 'Xiang Wang had the ambition to swallow mountains and rivers,' a metaphor for grandeur and ambition.
  • 咸阳三月火，骸骨乱入如麻 - 'The burning of Xiangyang for three months, bones scattered like hemp,' a poetic description of the devastation of the Qin capital.
  • 哭声惨怛天日 - 'Cries so sorrowful that they moved heaven and earth,' a hyperbolic expression of grief.
  • 眉容不敛 - 'His brow did not furrow,' indicating emotional control.
  • 铁作心肝者 - 'One with a heart of iron,' a metaphor for emotional hardness.
  • 垓下诀别之际 - 'At the moment of parting at Gaixia,' referring to the final battle of Xiang Yu.
  • 宝区血庙 - 'Precious regions and bloodstained temples,' a poetic reference to sacred or historical sites.
  • 了不经意 - 'Without a second thought,' indicating indifference.
  • 惟眷眷一妇人 - 'Only devoted to one woman,' showing emotional attachment.
  • 悲歌怅饮 - 'Sang mournfully and drank sorrowfully,' a poetic expression of grief.
  • 情不自禁 - 'Could not help but feel emotion,' a common expression of emotional response.
  • 高帝非天人欤 - 'Was the High Emperor not a divine being?' A rhetorical question about Liu Bang.
  • 能决意于太公、吕后，而不能决意于戚夫人 - 'He could make decisions about his father-in-law and wife, but not about his concubine,' indicating emotional conflict.
  • 杯羹可分，则笑嫚自若 - 'When the soup was divided, he laughed and acted as usual,' referring to a historical anecdote of Liu Bang being willing to sacrifice his family for power.
  • 羽翼已成，则欷歔不止 - 'When his power was fully formed, he wept endlessly,' indicating regret or sorrow.
  • 尤物移人 - 'Beautiful things can move people,' a classical expression referring to the power of beauty or desire to influence human behavior.
  • 虽大智大勇不能勉 - 'Even the wisest and bravest cannot resist,' indicating the inevitability of human desire.
  • 信哉 - 'Indeed,' a concluding exclamation of affirmation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 胡澹庵 - Hu Dan’an (胡铨), a Song dynasty official known for his opposition to the Jurchen and his exile. He is also known for his poetry and writings.
  • 朱元晦 - Zhu Xi (朱熹), a Neo-Confucian philosopher and scholar, often referred to as Zhu Yuanhui in classical texts.
  • 东坡《志林》 - *Zhi Lin* (Record of Events) by Su Shi (苏轼), also known as Su Dongpo, a famous Song dynasty poet and scholar.
  • 张元忠 - Zhang Yuanzhong, a historical figure who made a commentary on Su Bao (苏武).
  • 苏子卿 - Su Ziqing (苏武), a Han dynasty envoy to the Xiongnu, known for his endurance and loyalty.
  • 胡妇 - A woman of the Hu (胡) people, often used in classical texts to refer to northern ethnic groups or foreign women.
  • 文公 - A title for Zhu Xi, who is sometimes referred to as 'Wen Gong' (文公) in honorific contexts.
  • 郑叔友 - Zheng Shuyou (郑叔友), a Song dynasty scholar and commentator.
  • 刘、项 - Liu Bang (刘邦) and Xiang Yu (项羽), the two rival warlords who contended for the throne after the fall of the Qin dynasty.
  • 项王 - Xiang Yu, known as Xiang Wang, a legendary warlord of the late Qin and early Han periods.
  • 咸阳 - Xianyang, the capital of the Qin dynasty, destroyed during the fall of the Qin.
  • 垓下 - Gaixia, the site of Xiang Yu’s final defeat by Liu Bang.
  • 高帝 - Liu Bang, the founder of the Han dynasty, often referred to as 'Gao Di' (高帝).
  • 太公 - Liu Bang’s father, also a title for the father of a king.
  • 吕后 - Empress Lu (吕后), Liu Bang’s wife and later regent.
  • 戚夫人 - Qi Fu Ren (戚夫人), Liu Bang’s favorite concubine.
  • 尤物移人 - A classical expression referring to the power of beautiful or desirable things to sway human judgment or behavior.
  • 人欲险 - 'The danger of human desire,' a key concept in Neo-Confucian philosophy, particularly in Zhu Xi’s writings.

[674]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 惜别《长相思》辞 - A farewell poem titled 'Chang Xiang Si' (Longing for Each Other), a common title for love poems in Classical Chinese.
  • 吴山青，越山青 - A poetic device using repetition to emphasize the beauty and continuity of the landscape.
  • 两岸青山相送迎 - Describes the mountains on both sides of the river as if they are greeting and sending off the traveler.
  • 谁知离别情 - A rhetorical question expressing the depth of the separation.
  • 君泪盈，妾泪盈 - Describes the tears of both the man and the woman, emphasizing their mutual sorrow.
  • 罗带同心结未成 - Refers to the uncompleted knot of the sash, symbolizing the incomplete union.
  • 江头潮已平 - Indicates the calmness of the tide at the river mouth, often used to symbolize the passage of time or the end of an era.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 林君复（名逋，赐号和靖处士） - Lin Junfu, also known as Lin Bu, was a famous poet and recluse during the Song Dynasty. He was granted the title 'Hejing Scholar' by the court.
  • 宋史 - The official history of the Song Dynasty, which records Lin Bu's life and works.
  • 林洪著《家山清话》 - Lin Hong wrote a book titled 'Jia Shan Qing Hua' (Clear Conversations of the Family Mountain), which provides insights into his family and life.
  • 先人和靖先生 - Refers to Lin Bu as the father or ancestor of Lin Hong.
  • 李卫公 - Li Weigong, a military strategist and statesman during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 卫公李靖 - Li Jing, a famous general and strategist during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 亡妓谢秋娘 - A courtesan named Xie Qiuniang who died, and for whom Li Jing composed a song.
  • 《望江南》曲 - A type of song or poem, often associated with longing or nostalgia.
  • 《梦江南》 - Another name for the same type of song or poem.
  • 白乐天 - Bai Juyi, a renowned poet of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 忆江南 - A famous series of poems by Bai Juyi, also known as 'Reminiscences of Jiangnan.'

Proper Nouns

  • 和靖处士 - The title 'Hejing Scholar' granted to Lin Bu by the court.
  • 宋史 - The official history of the Song Dynasty.
  • 林洪 - A writer who documented his family's history.
  • 家山清话 - The title of Lin Hong's book.
  • 先人和靖先生 - Lin Bu, referred to as the father or ancestor of Lin Hong.
  • 李卫公 - Li Weigong, a military strategist and statesman during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 卫公李靖 - Li Jing, a famous general and strategist during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 亡妓谢秋娘 - A courtesan named Xie Qiuniang who died.
  • 望江南 - A type of song or poem.
  • 梦江南 - Another name for the same type of song or poem.
  • 白乐天 - Bai Juyi, a renowned poet of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 忆江南 - A famous series of poems by Bai Juyi.
  • 乐府杂录 - A collection of songs and poems.

[675]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 范文正守鄱阳 - Fan Zhongyan served as governor of Poyang; '守' means 'to serve as governor,' and '鄱阳' refers to Poyang, a city in Jiangxi Province.
  • 喜乐籍一小鬟 - He took pleasure in registering or acquiring a young maid; '喜乐籍' implies indulgence, and '一小鬟' refers to a young maid or servant girl.
  • 未几召还 - Not long after, he was summoned back to the capital; '未几' means 'not long after,' and '召还' means 'to be summoned back.'
  • 作诗寄后政 - He composed a poem to send to his successor; '作诗' means 'to compose a poem,' and '寄后政' means 'to send to the successor.'
  • 管领回 - To be entrusted to the care of the east wind; '管领' means 'to take care of,' and '回' means 'to return' or 'to bring back.'
  • 以胭脂寄其人 - He sent lipstick to the person; '以' means 'to send,' '胭脂' refers to lipstick or rouge, and '寄其人' means 'to send to the person.'
  • 纷纷坠叶飘香砌 - Leaves fall纷纷 and fragrance wafts through the courtyard; '纷纷' means 'falling纷纷ly,' '坠叶' means 'fallen leaves,' '飘' means 'waft,' and '香砌' refers to 'fragrant courtyard.'
  • 珍珠帘卷玉楼空 - The pearl curtains are rolled up, and the jade tower is empty; '珍珠帘' refers to 'pearl curtains,' '卷' means 'rolled up,' '玉楼' means 'jade tower,' and '空' means 'empty.'
  • 愁肠已断无由醉 - My sorrow has broken my heart, and I cannot drink to forget; '愁肠' means 'sorrowful heart,' '已断' means 'broken,' '无由' means 'no way,' and '醉' means 'to drink to forget.'
  • 残灯明灭枕头欹 - The dimming lamp flickers, and the pillow is tilted; '残灯' means 'dimming lamp,' '明灭' means 'flickering,' and '枕头欹' means 'tilted pillow.'
  • 谙尽孤眠滋味 - I have experienced the taste of solitary sleep; '谙尽' means 'experienced fully,' '孤眠' means 'solitary sleep,' and '滋味' means 'taste' or 'experience.'
  • 都来此事 - All this matter; '都来' means 'all this,' and '此事' means 'this matter.'
  • 眉间心上 - Between the brows and in the heart; '眉间' means 'between the brows,' and '心上' means 'in the heart.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 范文正 - Refers to Fan Zhongyan (989–1052), a prominent statesman, writer, and philosopher during the Song Dynasty.
  • 鄱阳 - Poyang, a city in Jiangxi Province, where Fan Zhongyan served as governor.
  • 庆朔堂 - A specific location or building, possibly a hall or pavilion, where Fan Zhongyan planted flowers.
  • 西溪丛语 - A historical text or collection of anecdotes, possibly referring to a work that records the story of Fan Zhongyan's poem.
  • 文子悱 - A person or scholar who questioned the authenticity of the story, suggesting it might have been fabricated by those who envied Fan Zhongyan.
  • 天之风月，地之花柳，与人之歌舞 - A metaphorical expression that compares the natural beauty of the heavens, the earth, and human activities, suggesting that all three are essential for the completeness of the 'three talents' (天、地、人).
  • 朱良矩 - A person or scholar who made the statement about the three talents.
  • 杨用修 - A person or scholar to whom Zhu Liangju spoke.

Proper Nouns

  • 范文正 - Fan Zhongyan, a prominent statesman and writer during the Song Dynasty.
  • 鄱阳 - Poyang, a city in Jiangxi Province.
  • 庆朔堂 - A specific location or building, possibly a hall or pavilion.
  • 西溪丛语 - A historical text or collection of anecdotes.
  • 文子悱 - A person or scholar who questioned the authenticity of the story.
  • 朱良矩 - A person or scholar who made the statement about the three talents.
  • 杨用修 - A person or scholar to whom Zhu Liangju spoke.

[676]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 同幕以妓会饮僧房 - A colloquial phrase describing a social gathering involving a courtesan in a temple.
  • 使妓逾垣而去 - A phrase describing the act of forcing the courtesan to jump over a wall to leave.
  • 公度不可隐 - A phrase indicating that Sima Guang could not conceal the truth.
  • 集句戏之 - Refers to the practice of composing poetry by selecting lines from existing works.
  • 年去年来来去忙 - A line from a poem, possibly from the *Book of Songs*.
  • 暂偷闲卧老僧床 - A line from a poem, likely referring to a temporary rest.
  • 惊回一觉游仙梦 - A line from a poem, describing a dream of immortality.
  • 又逐流莺过短墙 - A line from a poem, describing the flight of a skylark.
  • 即席赋《西江月》词 - Refers to composing a poem in the *Xijiangyue* style on the spot.
  • 宝髻松松绾就 - A line from a poem, describing a loose bun.
  • 铅华淡淡妆成 - A line from a poem, describing a light makeup.
  • 红烟紫雾罩轻尘 - A line from a poem, describing a misty, colorful atmosphere.
  • 飞絮游丝无定 - A line from a poem, describing floating fluff and threads.
  • 相见争如不见 - A line from a poem, expressing the idea that it is better not to meet.
  • 有情还似无情 - A line from a poem, expressing the idea that being in love is like being indifferent.
  • 笙歌散后酒微醒 - A line from a poem, describing the aftermath of a musical performance.
  • 深院月明人静 - A line from a poem, describing a quiet courtyard under the moonlight.
  • 赵清献公帅蜀 - Refers to Zhao Qingxian, a high-ranking official who governed Shu (Sichuan).
  • 有妓戴杏花 - Describes a courtesan wearing a peach blossom.
  • 髻上杏花真有幸 - A line from a poem, expressing the idea that the courtesan is fortunate.
  • 枝头梅子岂无媒 - A line from a poem, expressing the idea that the courtesan has a matchmaker.
  • 逼晚 - Refers to the late evening.
  • 值宿老兵 - Refers to an elderly soldier on duty.
  • 二鼓 - Refers to the second watch of the night, around midnight.
  • 赵忭 - Another name for Zhao Qingxian.
  • 不得无礼 - A phrase indicating that Zhao Qingxian would not behave improperly.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 司马温公 - Sima Guang, a renowned historian and statesman, known for his work on the *Zizhi Tongjian*.
  • 王荆公 - Wang Anshi, a reformer and statesman, known for his controversial policies.
  • 《西江月》词 - A popular poetic form in the Song Dynasty, often used for lyrical and philosophical expressions.
  • 赵清献公 - Zhao Qingxian, a high-ranking official and governor of Shu (Sichuan), known for his integrity and administrative skills.
  • 杏花 - Peach blossoms, often used in poetry to symbolize beauty and spring.
  • 梅子 - Plum blossoms, often used in poetry to symbolize love and marriage.
  • 值宿老兵 - An elderly soldier on duty, reflecting the military structure of the time.
  • 二鼓 - The second watch of the night, a common time reference in classical Chinese literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 司马温公 - Sima Guang.
  • 王荆公 - Wang Anshi.
  • 赵清献公 - Zhao Qingxian.
  • 杨元素学士 - Yang Yuanxue, a scholar.
  • 赵忭 - Another name for Zhao Qingxian.

[677]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此老兵乃真道学 - This old veteran is truly a scholar of the Way or a moral scholar.
  • 中夜心动 - At midnight, my heart was moved; '中夜' means 'midnight,' and '心动' means 'emotionally stirred.'
  • 厉声自呼曰 - He loudly called out to himself, saying; '厉声' means 'loudly' or 'sharply,' and '自呼' means 'called out to himself.'
  • 张悦其人 - Zhang was pleased with the person; '悦' means 'pleased' or 'delighted,' and '其人' means 'the person.'
  • 政以能制，见其不能忘 - The governance was effective because he could control himself, but he saw that he could not forget; '政以能制' means 'governance was effective because he could control himself,' and '见其不能忘' means 'he saw that he could not forget.'
  • 赵阅道、张乖崖，皆能制其情者 - Zhao Yuedao and Zhang Guayi, both of whom could control their emotions; '皆能制其情者' means 'both of whom could control their emotions.'
  • 于席上赠官妓小英歌曰 - At a banquet, he gifted an official courtesan named Xiao Ying with a song, saying; '于席上' means 'at a banquet,' '赠' means 'gifted,' '官妓' means 'official courtesan,' and '小英歌' means 'a song for Xiao Ying.'
  • 天教搏（抟）百媚，相映明如花 - Heaven taught her to possess a hundred graces, which shine as brightly as flowers; '天教' means 'Heaven taught,' '搏（抟）百媚' means 'possess a hundred graces,' and '相映明如花' means 'shine as brightly as flowers.'
  • 往近桃花坊正（北）面，门庭掩映如仙家 - She lived near the Peach Blossom Lane, with her door and courtyard hidden like those of a fairy; '往近' means 'lived near,' '桃花坊' means 'Peach Blossom Lane,' '正（北）面' means 'north side,' and '门庭掩映如仙家' means 'her door and courtyard hidden like those of a fairy.'
  • 美人宜称言不得，龙脑换（薰）衣香入骨 - A beautiful woman is not to be spoken of lightly, her clothes fragrant with dragon brain incense; '美人宜称言不得' means 'a beautiful woman is not to be spoken of lightly,' and '龙脑换（薰）衣香入骨' means 'her clothes fragrant with dragon brain incense.'
  • 维扬软縠如云英，亳郡轻纱似蝉翼 - The soft silk from Weiyang is as light as clouds, and the light gauze from Bozhou is as thin as cicada wings; '维扬软縠' means 'soft silk from Weiyang,' '如云英' means 'as light as clouds,' '亳郡轻纱' means 'light gauze from Bozhou,' and '似蝉翼' means 'as thin as cicada wings.'
  • 我疑天上婺女星之精，偷入筵中名小英 - I suspect that she is the essence of the Weaving Maid Star, who stole into the banquet and was named Xiao Ying; '我疑' means 'I suspect,' '天上婺女星之精' means 'the essence of the Weaving Maid Star,' '偷入筵中' means 'stole into the banquet,' and '名小英' means 'was named Xiao Ying.'
  • 又疑王母侍儿初失意，谪向人间为歌妓 - I also suspect that she is a fairy maid from the Queen Mother of the West who lost favor and was exiled to the mortal world as a singing girl; '又疑' means 'I also suspect,' '王母侍儿' means 'fairy maid from the Queen Mother of the West,' '初失意' means 'lost favor,' '谪向人间' means 'exiled to the mortal world,' and '为歌妓' means 'as a singing girl.'
  • 不然何得肤如红玉初碾成，眼似秋波双脸横 - Otherwise, how could she have skin as red as newly polished jade and eyes like autumn waves across her face? '不然何得' means 'otherwise, how could she have,' '肤如红玉初碾成' means 'skin as red as newly polished jade,' and '眼似秋波双脸横' means 'eyes like autumn waves across her face.'
  • 舞态因风欲飞去，歌声遏云长且清 - Her dancing posture, swayed by the wind, seemed to want to fly away, and her singing voice stopped the clouds, long and clear; '舞态因风欲飞去' means 'her dancing posture, swayed by the wind, seemed to want to fly away,' and '歌声遏云长且清' means 'her singing voice stopped the clouds, long and clear.'
  • 有时歌罢下香砌，几人魂魄遥相惊 - Sometimes, after she finished singing, she stepped down from the fragrant steps, and several people's souls were startled from afar; '有时歌罢' means 'sometimes, after she finished singing,' '下香砌' means 'stepped down from the fragrant steps,' and '几人魂魄遥相惊' means 'several people's souls were startled from afar.'

  • 在蜀娶婢，三年后归其父，犹然完璧 - Narrative describing a man who married a servant girl in Sichuan, returned her to her father after three years, and she remained intact, implying fidelity or purity.
  • 此诗亦靖节《闲情》、广平《梅花》之意也 - Refers to the style and content of the poem being similar to those of Tao Yuanming's 'Idle Sentiments' and Li Bai's 'Plum Blossoms,' both known for their themes of simplicity and natural beauty.
  • 然《岁华纪丽》称浣花小游江起于公，盖亦不厌游戏云 - Indicates that the 'Records of the Beautiful Years' attributes the origin of a small tour on the River to this person, suggesting a playful or recreational activity.
  • 柳外轻雷池上雨，雨声滴碎荷声 - Poetic description of the sound of rain on the lotus leaves, creating a delicate and evocative image.
  • 小楼西角断虹明 - Describes a rainbow appearing at the western corner of a small pavilion, adding a visual element to the scene.
  • 阑杆倚遍，佇待月华生 - The poet leans on the railing, waiting for the moonlight to shine.
  • 燕子飞来栖画栋，玉钩垂下帘旌 - Describes swallows flying to rest on the painted beams and the hanging curtain being lowered.
  • 凉波不动簟纹平 - Describes the calm water reflecting the pattern of the mat, creating a serene and peaceful atmosphere.
  • 水晶双枕，旁有堕钗横 - Describes the clear crystal pillows and a fallen hairpin lying beside them, adding a touch of elegance and intimacy.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 风惊绿 - Poetic expression referring to a green object (like leaves or grass) being startled or disturbed by the wind, conveying a sense of movement or agitation.
  • 若待得君来向此 - Conditional phrase meaning 'if you were to come here,' using '若' (if) and '得' (to obtain or arrive) to create a hypothetical scenario.
  • 花前对酒不忍触 - Poetic phrase meaning 'unable to bear touching the flowers while drinking wine,' conveying a sense of delicate emotion or hesitation.
  • 共粉泪，雨簌簌 - Metaphorical expression where '粉泪' (powder tears) refers to tears mixed with makeup, and '雨簌簌' (rain dripping) describes the sound of tears falling, evoking a scene of weeping.
  • 米元章有洁癖 - Straightforward statement that 'Mi Yuanzhang has a compulsion for cleanliness,' using the modern term '洁癖' (compulsion for cleanliness) for clarity.
  • 或言其矫 - Phrase meaning 'some say it is feigned' or 'some claim it is exaggerated,' suggesting that the cleanliness is not entirely genuine.
  • 家多声伎 - Phrase meaning 'has many musicians and performers,' referring to entertainers often in a household setting.
  • 使数卒解衣袒臂，奉其酒馔 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'had several soldiers undress and bare their arms to serve the wine and food,' emphasizing the soldiers' service.
  • 姬侍环于他客，杯盘狼藉 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the concubines surrounded the other guests, and the cups and plates were in disarray,' conveying a chaotic scene.
  • 久之，亦自迁坐于众宾之间 - Phrase meaning 'after a while, he also moved to sit among the other guests,' indicating a passage of time.
  • 相传有洁癖者，米元章、倪元镇二人 - Phrase meaning 'it is said that two people with a compulsion for cleanliness are Mi Yuanzhang and Ni Yuancheng,' indicating a traditional belief.
  • 元镇于女色，少所当意 - Phrase meaning 'Ni Yuancheng had few women who pleased him,' suggesting a lack of satisfaction.
  • 俾之浴 - Phrase meaning 'had her bathe,' indicating a command or request.
  • 复俾浴 - Phrase meaning 'had her bathe again,' emphasizing the action.
  • 东方既白，不复作巫山之梦 - Phrase meaning 'when the east began to turn white, he no longer dreamed of the mountains of Wu,' referring to the early morning and a metaphor for sexual dreams.
  • 情主人曰 - Phrase meaning 'the master of emotions said,' using a metaphorical title for someone who is deeply emotional.
  • 元章之癖，不胜其情 - Phrase meaning 'Mi Yuanzhang's compulsion could not withstand his emotions,' suggesting that his compulsion was overwhelmed by his feelings.
  • 元镇之情，不胜其癖 - Phrase meaning 'Ni Yuancheng's emotions could not withstand his compulsion,' suggesting that his emotions were overwhelmed by his compulsion.
  • 且其不能忘情则一也 - Phrase meaning 'and their inability to forget their emotions is the same,' suggesting a shared emotional attachment.
  • 王昆之回面，避妓也 - Phrase meaning 'Wang Kun turned away, avoiding the courtesan,' describing a physical action.
  • 陈烈之逾墙，逃妓也 - Phrase meaning 'Chen Lie climbed over the wall, escaping the courtesan,' describing a physical action.
  • 杨忠襄之焚衣，誓妓也 - Phrase meaning 'Yang Zhongxiang burned his clothes, vowing to avoid the courtesan,' describing a symbolic action.
  • 徵仲之弄臭脚，果以求脱妓也 - Phrase meaning 'Zhengzhong played with his smelly feet, indeed seeking to escape the courtesan,' describing a humorous and exaggerated physical action.
  • 是皆情之至者，诚虑忽不自制，故预违之 - Phrase meaning 'all of these are examples of extreme emotion, truly fearing a sudden loss of self-control, so they acted in advance to avoid it,' suggesting a deep emotional response.
  • 鲁男子之情，十倍于柳下惠 - Phrase meaning 'the emotion of the man from Lu is ten times that of Liu Xiaohui,' using historical figures to suggest a strong emotional contrast.
  • 伊川之强制，万不若明道先生 - Phrase meaning 'the forced restraint of Yichuan is not even a thousandth of Mingdao Master,' using historical figures to suggest a strong contrast in emotional control.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 黄鲁直尝谪涪州，因称涪翁 — This phrase introduces Huang Tingjian's nickname 'Fuzhou Old Man' due to his exile to Fuzhou.
  • 泸帅留府 — This phrase indicates that the military governor of Luzhou invited Huang to stay at his residence.
  • 料得有心怜宋玉，只因无奈楚襄何 — This line is ambiguous in its interpretation. It could mean that the courtesan has feelings for Huang but is constrained by her situation, similar to how Cao Xueqin (宋玉) might have been constrained by King Xiang (楚襄).
  • 暂来低处立功名 — This line suggests that the courtesan will achieve fame and success in a humble position.
  • 虽然不作擎天柱 — This line suggests that despite not being a pillar that supports the sky, the courtesan still has value and purpose.

  • 什聚針盈缽 - Kumarajiva gathered needles in a bowl, a metaphorical act to demonstrate his spiritual purity.
  • 舉匕不異常食 - He lifted a spoon as if eating normally, suggesting he was unaffected by the needles.
  • 若能效我，乃可蓄室 - If you can imitate me, then you may keep a household, Kumarajiva's condition for monks who wanted to have families.
  • 一說興常謂什曰 - Another account says Yao Xing often told Kumarajiva, introducing an alternative version of events.
  • 大師聰明超悟 - Master, your intelligence and insight surpass all others, praising Kumarajiva's intellect.
  • 天下莫二 - No one in the world can match you, emphasizing Kumarajiva's exceptional status.
  • 若一旦厭世 - If you ever tire of the world, referring to the possibility of Kumarajiva renouncing his life.
  • 何可令法種無嗣 - How can we allow the lineage of the Dharma to die out, Yao Xing's concern about the continuation of Kumarajiva's teachings.
  • 遂以妓女十人 - So he offered ten courtesans, indicating Yao Xing's provision of women for Kumarajiva.
  • 逼令受之 - Forced him to accept them, suggesting coercion.
  • 自是別立廨舍 - From then on, he established a separate residence, indicating Kumarajiva's separation from the monastic community.
  • 不住僧房 - He no longer stayed in the monastic quarters, further emphasizing his separation.
  • 習靜業於山寺有年矣 - Practiced meditation in a mountain temple for many years, describing the monk's long-term practice.
  • 忽見一少婦喪夫來山求薦 - Suddenly saw a young widow who had lost her husband come to the mountain seeking help, describing the encounter.
  • 僧睹之，不覺動念 - The monk saw her and was unconsciously moved, indicating his reaction.
  • 既去，而日夕思之不忘 - After she left, he thought of her day and night without forgetting, describing the monk's persistent thoughts.
  • 數月，左股內隱隱聞嬰兒啼聲 - After several months, he heard faint baby cries inside his left thigh, a description of the monk's miraculous conception.
  • 久之，右股亦然 - After a long time, the same happened to his right thigh, indicating the second conception.
  • 大怖 - Greatly terrified, describing the monk's reaction to the miraculous conception.
  • 以為業緣所召 - Thinking it was due to karmic connections, explaining the monk's reasoning.
  • 遂還俗，娶其婦為妻 - He then returned to secular life and married the woman, describing the monk's decision.
  • 二年連得二子 - Two years later, he had two sons, describing the outcome of the monk's marriage.
  • 更十餘年 - After another ten years, indicating the passage of time.
  • 忽念此身墮落 - Suddenly realized this body had fallen, describing the monk's spiritual awakening.
  • 勸妻同出家於寺 - Persuaded his wife to leave home and join a temple, describing the monk's decision to renounce his life.
  • 以二子與人為奴 - Gave his two sons to others as slaves, describing the monk's decision to renounce his children.
  • 及入山，眾憎厭惡逐之 - When he entered the mountain, the other monks disliked and drove him away, describing the monk's rejection by the monastic community.
  • 遂習禪於白蛇洞中 - He then practiced Zen in the White Snake Cave, describing the monk's new practice location.
  • 久之，白蛇俯首以避，虎至，伏洞門不敢仰視 - After a long time, the white snake bowed its head to avoid him, and when a tiger came, it crouched at the cave entrance and dared not look up, describing the monk's spiritual power.
  • 遂乘虎至寺，眾僧競觀 - He then rode the tiger to the temple, and the monks all watched, describing the monk's dramatic return.
  • 口占一偈云 - Recited a verse spontaneously, indicating the monk's final act before his death.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening sentence introduces Kumarajiva and his status, but the structure is somewhat elliptical.
  • The monk's request is presented in a direct speech format, but the context of the request is unclear.
  • This sentence describes a miraculous conception, but the exact nature of the event is ambiguous.
  • This sequence of actions and words is presented in a compressed form, requiring careful parsing to understand the sequence of events.
  • This alternative account is presented in a continuous narrative, but the sequence of Yao Xing's reasoning and actions is complex.
  • This long narrative describes the second monk's journey, from his initial encounter to his final act of reciting a verse. The sequence of events is detailed but requires careful parsing to understand the monk's progression.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 两峰相对叠晴霞 - Poetic description of two peaks facing each other with the sun setting, creating a colorful sky.
  • 涧底泉香泛落花 - Describes the fragrance of spring water in a stream, mixed with the scent of fallen flowers.
  • 埋却袈裟离世网 - Literally means 'burying the robe to escape the world's web,' a metaphor for renouncing worldly affairs and becoming a monk.
  • 寄生二子在人家 - Means 'leaving two sons in someone's house,' suggesting that the monk left his children with others before becoming a monk.
  • 神通骑出斑斑虎 - Refers to a supernatural ability to ride a tiger, a common motif in Buddhist and Taoist stories symbolizing the control over fear and danger.
  • 感应呼来白白蛇 - Means 'calling a white snake through感应 (感应),' which suggests a spiritual connection or divine intervention.
  • 是圣是凡君莫测 - Means 'whether he is a saint or an ordinary person, you cannot tell,' indicating the monk's mysterious and possibly supernatural qualities.
  • 相逢休笑亦休夸 - Means 'when you meet him, do not laugh or boast,' suggesting a humble and respectful attitude towards the monk.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 僧知业 - Refers to a monk named Zhiye, known for his high moral character and poetic talent.
  • 陆鲁望（龟蒙） - Refers to Lu Wugong (Lu Guimeng), a famous poet and scholar of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 蒋氏 - Refers to Lady Jiang, the wife of Lu Wugong, who was known for her love of drinking.
  • 接垒桥通何处路，倚栏人是阿谁家 - A line from Zhiye's poem, describing a bridge connecting unknown paths and a person leaning on a railing, whose home is unknown. This line reflects the monk's poetic and philosophical nature.
  • 圣保寺 - Refers to Shengbao Temple, a temple where Zhiye lived and practiced.
  • 沈石田 - Refers to Shen Zhou, a renowned painter and calligrapher of the Ming Dynasty.
  • 名妓 - Refers to a famous courtesan, often associated with high social status and artistic talent.
  • 菜花蛱蝶图 - Refers to a painting of rapeseed flowers and butterflies, a common subject in Chinese art.
  • 桃花生子菜花台（薹），细雨蛙声出草莱 - A line from the poem, describing the growth of peach blossoms and rapeseed flowers, and the sound of frogs in the grass, evoking a peaceful and natural scene.
  • 一段春光多不见，却教蛱蝶误飞来 - A line from the poem, suggesting that much of the spring beauty is unseen, but butterflies are drawn to it, symbolizing the unexpected and fleeting nature of beauty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 西廂 - Refers to *The Western Chamber* (*西廂記*), a classic Chinese play by Wang Shifu, which tells the story of Zhang Sheng and Cui Yingying.
  • 空門 - A Buddhist term for monastic life, often used to describe monks or monasteries.
  • 悟禪 - Refers to gaining insight into Zen Buddhism, a school of Mahayana Buddhism that emphasizes meditation and direct experience of enlightenment.
  • 風流之士 - Refers to a man of refined and cultured tastes, often associated with literary or artistic pursuits.
  • 風流身 - Literally 'a body of elegance,' used here to describe the monk's demeanor or appearance.
  • 瀨女 - A term referring to a woman who lived near a river or waterfall, often used in classical Chinese literature to describe a woman of purity or virtue.
  • 伍胥 - Refers to Wu Zixu, a historical figure from the Spring and Autumn period who is famous for his loyalty and tragic fate.
  • 違父兄之難 - Refers to Wu Zixu's decision to leave his father and brothers during a time of political turmoil.
  • 潛行至吳 - Refers to Wu Zixu's secret journey to the state of Wu, where he sought refuge and later played a key role in the downfall of the state of Chu.
  • 乞食溧陽 - Refers to Wu Zixu's time of hardship, when he was reduced to begging for food in Liyang.
  • 擊綿於瀨水之上 - Refers to the act of pounding flax by the side of the Lishui River, a common activity for women in ancient China.
  • 筥中有飯 - Refers to the basket containing rice, a staple food in ancient China.
  • 振窮途少飯 - Refers to the act of giving a little food to someone in dire straits, a gesture of kindness and compassion.
  • 長跪而與之 - Refers to the act of kneeling and giving something to someone, a gesture of respect and humility.
  • 再餐而止 - Refers to the act of eating twice and then stopping, indicating a sense of restraint or satisfaction.
  • 掩夫人之壺漿 - Refers to the act of covering the lady's drink, a gesture of respect and care.
  • 自投於瀨水 - Refers to the act of throwing oneself into the Lishui River, a tragic act of suicide.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 子猶曰 - Conventional phrase meaning 'Zi You said,' introducing a quotation from the text *Zi You*.
  • 同一識英雄俊眼 - Ambiguous phrase; could mean 'to recognize heroes with keen eyes' or 'to have the same insight as heroes.'
  • 幸則為紅拂妓，雄服連辔 - Phrase using the story of Hong Fu to describe a fortunate outcome; '紅拂妓' refers to Hong Fu, a legendary courtesan who became the wife of Li Yuanji.
  • 不幸則為擊绵女，寒风濑水 - Phrase using a contrasting story to describe a tragic fate; '擊绵女' symbolizes a tragic and isolated fate.
  • 或言此女可以无死，甚不然也 - Phrase translating to 'some say this woman can avoid death, but that is very wrong.'
  • 田光先生有云 - Conventional phrase meaning 'Mr. Tian Guang said,' introducing a quotation from Mr. Tian Guang.
  • 长者为行，不使人疑 - Phrase translating to 'a virtuous person acts in a way that does not cause suspicion.'
  • 掩夫人之壶浆，勿令其露 - Phrase translating to 'cover the lady's wine and food, do not let it be seen.'
  • 此女不死，子胥虽行，终未释然也 - Phrase translating to 'if this woman does not die, even if Sun Bin goes, he will not be at ease.'
  • 知礼义之不可越亏，而犹然跪进壶浆，劝勉加餐 - Phrase translating to 'knowing that propriety and righteousness cannot be violated, yet still kneeling and offering wine and food, encouraging and urging to eat more.'
  • 独念子胥非恒人故耳 - Phrase translating to 'only thinking that Sun Bin is not an ordinary person.'
  • 既知其非恒人，亦何惜一死，以安其魂，而定其事乎 - Phrase translating to 'if one knows that he is not an ordinary person, why not sacrifice one's life to rest his soul and settle the matter.'
  • 此女虽终身不嫁，冥冥之中，固已嫁子胥矣 - Phrase translating to 'even if this woman never marries in this life, in the afterlife, she has already married Sun Bin.'

  • 田光先生 - Historical figure known for his wisdom and advice to Prince Dan of Yan; famous for his story of sacrificing himself to help Prince Dan.
  • 礼义 - Refers to propriety and righteousness, core values in Confucian ethics.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 飛燕緣主家大人 - A phrase indicating that Feiyan gained entry to the palace by virtue of her connection to the imperial family. '緣' means 'by virtue of' or 'through the favor of.'
  • 得入宮召幸 - A standard expression meaning 'was summoned into the palace for imperial favor' or 'was called into the palace to be favored by the emperor.'
  • 其姑妹樊嬺 - '姑妹' refers to 'sister-in-law' (the wife of the husband's brother) or 'nephew's wife' depending on context. Here, it likely means 'her sister' or 'her sister-in-law,' as '樊嬺' is identified as a relative of Feiyan.
  • 為丞光司帟者 - A title or position, likely referring to someone who served in the palace as a chief of the imperial court's ceremonial or administrative staff. '司帟' could be a term for managing court affairs or managing the imperial canopy (帟, a canopy or tent used in court rituals).
  • 故識飛燕與射鳥兒事 - '故識' means 'knew beforehand' or 'had prior knowledge of.' '射鳥兒' is a name, likely a servant or a concubine who was involved in a previous incident with Feiyan.
  • 為之寒心 - A phrase meaning 'made her feel cold-hearted' or 'caused her to feel sorrowful or fearful.'
  • 及幸 - A concise phrase meaning 'when she was favored' or 'when she was summoned for the emperor’s pleasure.'
  • 瞑目牢握，涕交颐下 - A vivid description: 'closed her eyes tightly, clutched [the emperor] with both hands, tears flowed down her cheeks.'
  • 戰栗不迎帝 - 'Trembled and did not welcome the emperor' – indicating fear or shyness.
  • 帝拥飞燕三夕，不能接 - The emperor embraced Feiyan for three nights, but could not consummate the union. '不能接' here likely refers to the inability to have sexual intercourse.
  • 略无谴意 - 'Had no intention of blaming her' or 'showed no anger.'
  • 宫中素幸者 - 'Those who had been favored by the emperor before.'
  • 丰若有余，柔若无骨 - A poetic description of Feiyan’s beauty and grace: 'full and abundant, yet soft as if without bones.'
  • 迁延谦畏，若远若近 - 'Hesitant and modest, as if distant yet near' – describing her demeanor.
  • 礼义人也 - 'A person of propriety and righteousness.'
  • 宁与汝曹婢胁肩者比耶 - A rhetorical question: 'Would you rather compare her to those婢胁肩 (servants who fawn and flatter)?'
  • 流丹浃席 - 'Red blood flowed and soaked the mat' – likely a metaphorical expression for intense physical passion or emotional response.
  • 私语飞燕曰 - 'Whispered privately to Feiyan.'
  • 射鸟者不近汝邪 - 'Didn't the 'bird-shooter' (射鸟儿) get close to you?' – likely a sarcastic or questioning remark about her previous relationship.
  • 内视三日，肉肌盈实矣 - 'I looked inward for three days, and my flesh and muscles became full and solid.' – possibly a metaphor for physical or emotional exhaustion or recovery.
  • 帝体洪壮，创我甚焉 - 'The emperor's body is strong and robust, and it caused great injury to me.' – indicating physical pain or trauma.
  • 自此特幸后宫，号赵皇后 - 'From this point on, she was especially favored in the harem, and was called the Queen Dowager Zhao.' '号' here means 'was given the title' or 'was known as.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 丞光司帟者 - A title likely referring to a high-ranking official in the palace, possibly a chief administrator or overseer of ceremonial matters. '丞光' may be a title, and '司帟' may refer to managing the imperial canopy or courtly arrangements.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 为后别开远条馆 - A phrase meaning 'to build a separate pavilion for the empress,' which is a metaphorical expression indicating the construction of a special residence for the empress.
  • 赐紫茸云气帐，文玉几，赤金九层博山缘合 - Describes the luxurious gifts given to the empress, including a purple velvet cloud-patterned canopy, an inlaid jade table, and a nine-layered incense burner with a red gold rim. These items are symbols of imperial favor and luxury.
  • 讽后曰 - Means 'advised the empress,' where '讽' implies a subtle or indirect suggestion.
  • 上久无子，宫中不思千万岁计耶 - Translates to 'The emperor has been without an heir for a long time; isn't the palace thinking about the long-term future of the dynasty?' It reflects a concern about the continuation of the imperial lineage.
  • 何不时进上，求有子 - Means 'Why don't you sometimes present yourself to the emperor to seek an heir?' This is a direct suggestion to the empress to take action to ensure the continuation of the royal line.
  • 辅属体，无所不靡 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'to support the body in a way that nothing can resist,' which implies a state of utmost comfort and pleasure.
  • 谓为温柔乡 - Means 'called it a land of gentleness,' where '温柔乡' is a poetic term for a place of comfort and pleasure.
  • 吾老是乡矣，不能效武皇帝求白云乡也 - Translates to 'I am old and content here, I cannot emulate Emperor Wu's pursuit of the White Clouds.' This reflects the emperor's satisfaction with his current situation and a contrast to the legendary pursuits of past emperors.
  • 贺曰 - Means 'congratulated,' indicating a formal expression of approval or praise.
  • 陛下真得仙者 - Means 'Your Majesty is truly a divine being,' a hyperbolic expression of admiration.
  • 立赐嬺鲛文万金、锦二十四匹 - Means 'immediately bestowed on Fan Nuan ten thousand gold pieces and twenty-four bolts of brocade,' indicating the immediate reward for the advisor's suggestion.
  • 合德尤幸，号为赵婕妤 - Means 'He De was especially favored and was given the title of Zhao Jieyu,' indicating the high status and favor she received from the emperor.

  • 又為温室、疑釭室、浴兰室 - Also built a greenhouse, a *Yi Gang* room, and a *Yu Lan* room.

  • 后遂不报 - The concubine then did not respond.

  • 时下朱里芮姥者，求捕獭狸献 - At that time, an old woman from Zhu Li named Rui came forward to capture and offer the otters.
  • 姥谓姑曰：‘是狸不他食，当饭以鸭。’ - The old woman said to Lady Li, 'This *li* does not eat other things; it should be fed with ducks.'

  • 今夷术，真似此也 - Now this *Bo Yi* technique is truly like this.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 立 - Refers to being made empress.
  • 廢弑 - Compound verb meaning 'to depose and assassinate.'
  • 荒淫放恣 - Describes a state of debauchery, licentiousness, and unrestrained behavior.
  • 與太医令程据等乱 - Indicates an illicit relationship with the Imperial Physician Chen Ju and others.
  • 洛南有盗尉部小吏 - Refers to a minor official in the southern part of the city of Luoyang.
  • 端丽美容止 - Describes someone as beautiful and well-mannered.
  • 既给厮役 - Means 'having been assigned menial tasks.'
  • 忽有非常衣服 - Describes an unexpected change in attire.
  • 众咸疑其窃 - Means 'everyone suspected him of theft.'
  • 盗尉泰而辩之 - Indicates that the official, named Tai, defended or clarified the matter.
  • 贾后疏亲欲求盗物 - Refers to distant relatives of Empress Jia who wanted to see the stolen items.
  • 往听对辞 - Means 'went to listen to the interrogation.'
  • 先行逢一老妪 - Describes meeting an old woman first.
  • 说家有疾病 - Means 'told that her family had an illness.'
  • 师卜云 - Refers to a diviner's prediction.
  • 宜得城南少年厌之 - Suggests that a young man from the south of the city should be used to appease the illness.
  • 欲暂相烦，必有重报 - Means 'wants to temporarily trouble you, and there will be a great reward.'
  • 上车下帷，内簏箱中 - Describes being placed inside a box on a carriage.
  • 可行十余里 - Indicates traveling about ten miles.
  • 过六七门限 - Means 'passed through six or seven thresholds.'
  • 开簏箱 - Describes opening the box.
  • 忽见楼阙好屋 - Refers to suddenly seeing beautiful buildings.
  • 问‘此是何处’ - Means 'asked where this was.'
  • 云‘是天上’ - The old woman replied that it was heaven.
  • 即以香汤见浴 - Describes being bathed in fragrant water.
  • 好衣美食 - Refers to being dressed in fine clothes and fed delicious food.
  • 将入，见一妇人 - Means 'as they were about to enter, they saw a woman.'
  • 年可二十五六 - Describes the woman as being around twenty-five or twenty-six years old.
  • 短形，青黑色眉，后有疵 - Describes the woman as having a short figure, black eyebrows, and a blemish on her face.
  • 见留数夕 - Means 'she kept him for several nights.'
  • 共寝欢宴 - Refers to sleeping together and having a feast.
  • 临出赠此众物 - Means 'on leaving, she gave him these many things.'
  • 听者闻其形状 - Refers to those who heard the description of the woman.
  • 知是贾后 - Indicates that they recognized the woman as Empress Jia.
  • 惭笑而去 - Means 'they felt ashamed and laughed as they left.'
  • 时他人入者多死 - Describes that many others who entered died.
  • 惟此小吏以后爱之，得全而出 - Indicates that only this minor official survived because Empress Jia loved him.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 郁林王 - Title indicating a king or prince, likely a regional ruler.
  • 何妃 - Title '妃' (fei) refers to a consort or wife of a king or prince.
  • 讳婧英 - '讳' (wei) is a respectful term used before a person's name, especially in historical contexts. '婧英' (Jingying) is the given name.
  • 庐江灊人 - '庐江' (Lujiang) is a place name, and '灊人' (Qianren) refers to someone from the area of Qian.
  • 抚军将军 - A military rank, equivalent to a general or marshal.
  • 戢女 - '戢' (ji) is the surname, and '女' (nü) indicates that she is a woman.
  • 南郡王妃 - The title of a consort for the Prince of Nanjun.
  • 文惠太子 - A title referring to the son of Emperor Wu of Liu Song, known as Emperor Ming of Liu Song.
  • 嫌戢无男 - '嫌' (xian) means to dislike or have a prejudice against, '无男' (wunan) means having no male heirs.
  • 门孤 - '门' (men) refers to family lineage or clan, and '孤' (gu) means solitary or without support.
  • 不欲与婚 - '不欲' (buyu) means unwilling, '与婚' (yuhun) means to marry.
  • 正俭以南郡王妃 - '正俭' (zhengjian) is a name, and '以' (yi) means because of or due to.
  • 便为将来外戚 - '便' (bian) means thus or therefore, '外戚' (waiqi) refers to relatives by marriage to the imperial family.
  • 惟须高胄 - '惟' (wei) means only or just, '高胄' (gaizhou) refers to noble or high-ranking lineage.
  • 荫华族弱 - '荫' (yin) means to shelter or protect, '华族' (huazu) refers to a prestigious family, '弱' (ruo) means weak or lacking strength.
  • 永明三年乃成婚 - '永明' (yongming) is the reign title of Emperor Wu of Liu Song, '三年' (san nian) means the third year, '乃' (nai) means then or finally, '成婚' (cheng hun) means to get married.
  • 禀性淫乱 - '禀性' (binxing) means innate nature, '淫乱' (yinluan) means licentious or promiscuous.
  • 南郡王所与无赖人游 - '南郡王' (Nanjun Wang) refers to the Prince of Nanjun, '所与' (suoyu) means those he associates with, '无赖人' (wulai ren) means disreputable or scoundrel.
  • 妃择其美者 - '妃' (fei) refers to the consort, '择' (ze) means to choose, '其美者' (qimeizhe) means those who are beautiful.
  • 皆与交欢 - '皆' (jie) means all, '与' (yu) means with, '交欢' (jiao huan) means to have sexual relations.
  • 南郡王侍书人马澄 - '南郡王' (Nanjun Wang) refers to the Prince of Nanjun, '侍书人' (shi shu ren) means a person who serves as a scribe or secretary, '马澄' (Ma Cheng) is the name.
  • 年少，色美甚 - '年少' (nianshao) means young, '色美甚' (semei shen) means very beautiful.
  • 妃悦之 - '妃' (fei) refers to the consort, '悦' (yue) means to be pleased or delighted, '之' (zhi) is a pronoun referring to the subject.
  • 常与斗腕较力 - '常' (chang) means often, '与' (yu) means with, '斗腕' (dou wan) means to wrestle or grapple, '较力' (jiaoli) means to compare strength.
  • 南郡王以为欢笑 - '南郡王' (Nanjun Wang) refers to the Prince of Nanjun, '以为' (yiwu) means to consider, '欢笑' (huaxiao) means to laugh or be amused.
  • 澄者，本剡县寒人 - '澄者' (cheng zhe) means the person named Ma Cheng, '本' (ben) means originally, '剡县' (Shanyan) is a place name, '寒人' (hanren) means a commoner or poor person.
  • 尝于南岸逼略人家女 - '尝' (chang) means once or at one time, '于' (yu) means at or in, '南岸' (nan'an) means the south bank, '逼略' (bi lue) means to forcibly take, '人家女' (renjia nü) means a woman from a household.
  • 为秣陵县所录 - '为' (wei) means to be, '秣陵县' (Moling Xian) is a place name, '所录' (suilu) means to be recorded or documented.
  • 南郡王语县散遣之 - '南郡王' (Nanjun Wang) refers to the Prince of Nanjun, '语' (yu) means to tell, '县' (xian) means the county, '散遣' (san qian) means to release or dismiss, '之' (zhi) is a pronoun referring to the subject.
  • 澄又逼求姨女为妾 - '澄' (cheng) refers to Ma Cheng, '又' (you) means again, '逼求' (bi qiu) means to forcibly request, '姨女' (yi nü) means the sister's daughter, '为妾' (wei qie) means to be a concubine.
  • 姨不与 - '姨' (yi) means aunt, '不与' (bu yu) means not to give.
  • 澄诣建康令涴徽孚讼之 - '澄' (cheng) refers to Ma Cheng, '诣' (yi) means to go to, '建康令' (Jiankang Ling) is the magistrate of Jiankang, '涴徽孚' (Wahui Fu) is the name of the magistrate, '讼之' (song zhi) means to sue or litigate against.
  • 徽孚曰 - '徽孚' (Wahui Fu) is the name of the magistrate, '曰' (yue) means said.
  • 姨女可为妇，不可为妾 - '姨女' (yi nü) means the sister's daughter, '可为妇' (ke wei fu) means can be a wife, '不可为妾' (bu ke wei qie) means cannot be a concubine.
  • 仆父为给事中 - '仆' (pu) is a humble term for 'I,' '父' (fu) means father, '为' (wei) means to be, '给事中' (gishizhong) is a high-ranking official.
  • 门户既成 - '门户' (menhu) means family or household, '既成' (ji cheng) means already established.
  • 姨家犹是贱 - '姨家' (yi jia) means the aunt's family, '犹是' (you shi) means still is, '贱' (jian) means lowly or inferior.
  • 正可为妾耳 - '正' (zheng) means just, '可为妾' (ke wei qie) means can be a concubine, '耳' (er) is a particle indicating a limitation.
  • 徽孚呵而遣之 - '徽孚' (Wahui Fu) is the name of the magistrate, '呵' (he) means to rebuke, '而' (er) is a conjunction, '遣之' (qian zhi) means to send away.
  • 十一年，为皇太孙妃 - '十一年' (shi nian) means the eleventh year, '为' (wei) means to be, '皇太孙' (huangtaisun) means the crown prince's son, '妃' (fei) means consort.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 北齊武成皇后胡氏 - Refers to the consort of Emperor Wu Cheng of the Northern Qi dynasty, whose family name is Hu.
  • 其母范陽盧道約女 - Indicates that the mother of the empress was the daughter of Lu Daoyue of Fanyang.
  • 产后主日 - Likely refers to the day after childbirth, or the day of the birth.
  • 鸮鸣于产帐上 - ‘An owl called on the birth canopy.’ The owl is a symbol of ill omen or death in Chinese culture.
  • 尊为皇太后 - ‘Was honored as empress dowager.’ A standard honorific title.
  • 陆媪及和士开，密谋杀赵郡王叡 - ‘Lü the old lady and He Shikai conspired in secret to kill Prince Zhaojun Rui.’ This is a standard historical narrative structure.
  • 出娄定远、高文遥为刺史 - ‘Exiled Lou Dingyuan and Gao Wenyao as prefects.’ This is a standard bureaucratic action in imperial China.
  • 和、陆谄事太后无不至 - ‘He and Lü flattered the empress dowager without limit.’ The phrase ‘无不至’ means ‘without limit’ or ‘to the extreme.’
  • 初武成时，后与诸阉人亵狎 - ‘In the early days of Wu Cheng, the empress was intimate with eunuchs.’ ‘亵狎’ means to be familiar or intimate in a sexual or improper way.
  • 武成宠幸和士开，每与后握槊 - ‘Wu Cheng favored He Shikai, and often played zhu shuo with the empress.’ Zhu shuo is a game or a form of gambling or entertainment with sticks.
  • 因此与后奸通 - ‘Therefore, he had an affair with the empress.’ A direct statement of infidelity.
  • 数出诣佛寺 - ‘Frequently visited Buddhist temples.’ Indicates religious or spiritual activity.
  • 又与沙门昙献通 - ‘Also had an affair with the monk Tanxian.’ ‘通’ here refers to an illicit relationship.
  • 布金钱于献席下 - ‘Scattered money under Tanxian’s mat.’ This is a metaphor for giving bribes or gifts.
  • 挂宝胡床于献屋壁，武成平日所御也 - ‘Hung a precious hu chuang (a type of chair or seat) on Tanxian’s wall; it was one that Wu Cheng used daily.’ This is a detailed description of a gift.
  • 乃置百僧于内殿，托以听讲 - ‘Then placed a hundred monks in the inner palace, under the pretense of listening to lectures.’ This is a euphemistic way of saying she was engaging in secret religious or sexual activities.
  • 日夜与昙献寝处 - ‘Slept with Tanxian day and night.’ A direct statement of a close, intimate relationship.
  • 以献为昭立统 - ‘Made Tanxian the leader of the Zhao Li Tong (probably a Buddhist sect or order).’ The term Zhao Li Tong is unclear, but likely refers to a religious or spiritual title.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 北齐 - Northern Qi, one of the northern dynasties during the Northern and Southern Dynasties period (420–589 CE).
  • 武成 - Emperor Wu Cheng, the third emperor of the Northern Qi dynasty (r. 561–565 CE).
  • 胡氏 - A common surname, but in this context refers to the consort of the emperor.
  • 安定 - A county in present-day Gansu Province, historically an important region.
  • 范阳 - A historical region in present-day Hebei Province, known for its aristocratic families.
  • 卢道约 - A historical figure, likely a noble or official in the Northern Qi court.
  • 陆媪 - Lü the old lady, likely a powerful female figure, possibly a concubine or relative of the emperor.
  • 和士开 - He Shikai, a eunuch and political figure who had significant influence in the Northern Qi court.
  • 赵郡王叡 - Prince Zhaojun Rui, a member of the imperial family, likely a rival or political threat.
  • 娄定远、高文遥 - Lou Dingyuan and Gao Wenyao, officials who were exiled or removed from power.
  • 阉人 - Eunuchs, common in Chinese imperial courts and often involved in court intrigue.
  • 握槊 - A game or form of entertainment, possibly a gambling game or a martial pastime. The term is obscure, but it was a common pastime in the imperial court.
  • 胡僧 - A foreign monk, possibly from Central Asia or India, common in Chinese Buddhist history.
  • 鸮 - An owl, often associated with death or bad omens in Chinese culture.
  • 产帐 - The canopy or tent used during childbirth, symbolizing the birth event.
  • 沙门 - A term for Buddhist monks or monastic practitioners.
  • 昙献 - A Buddhist monk, possibly a member of the Tiantai or Chan school. His name is likely historical.
  • 胡床 - A type of folding chair or seat, often associated with foreign or nomadic cultures. It was used in Chinese courts and homes.
  • 内殿 - The inner palace or inner court, where the emperor and empress resided.
  • 昭立统 - Uncertain, but likely refers to a religious title or leadership role in a Buddhist order.

Proper Nouns

  • 北齐 - Northern Qi, a historical dynasty.
  • 武成皇后胡氏 - Empress Dowager Hu of the Northern Qi.
  • 安定 - A county in present-day Gansu Province.
  • 胡延 - Hu Yan, the father of the empress.
  • 范阳 - A historical region in present-day Hebei Province.
  • 卢道约 - Lu Daoyue, the father of the empress’s mother.
  • 陆媪 - Lü the old lady, a powerful court figure.
  • 和士开 - He Shikai, a eunuch and court official.
  • 赵郡王叡 - Prince Zhaojun Rui, a member of the imperial family.
